title: Epitaph - On the Countess Dowager of Pembroke
content:
Underneath this sable herse
    Lies the subject of all verse:
    Sidney's sister, Pembroke's mother:
    Death, ere thou hast slain another,
    Fair and learn'd, and good as she,
    Time shall throw a dart at thee.

    Marble piles let no man raise
    To her name: for after days
    Some kind woman born as she,
    Reading this, like Niobe
    Shall turn marble, and become
    Both her mourner and her tomb.
meaning:
William Browne's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Epitaph - On the Countess Dowager of Pembroke"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
